From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American screenwriter (1922–2013)

For the British comics writer, see [Gerry Finley-Day](/wiki/Gerry_Finley-Day "Gerry Finley-Day").

| Gerry Day | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | Gerald Lallande Day  (1922-01-27)January 27, 1922  [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), [California](/wiki/California "California"), U.S. |
| Died | February 13, 2013(2013-02-13) (aged 91)  Los Angeles, California, U.S. |
| Resting place | [San Fernando Mission Cemetery](/wiki/San_Fernando_Mission_Cemetery "San Fernando Mission Cemetery") |
| Occupation | Screenwriter |
| Years active | 1954–1994 |
| Awards | * [Writers Guild of America Award](/wiki/Writers_Guild_of_America_Award "Writers Guild of America Award")   1967 *[Writers Guild of America Award for Television: Episodic Drama](/wiki/Writers_Guild_of_America_Award_for_Television:_Episodic_Drama "Writers Guild of America Award for Television: Episodic Drama")* – *[Court Martial](/wiki/Court_Martial_(TV_series) "Court Martial (TV series)")* episode "Judge Them Gently" * [Saturn Award](/wiki/Saturn_Award "Saturn Award")   1980 *[Best Writing](/wiki/7th_Saturn_Awards#Best_Writing "7th Saturn Awards")* – *[The Black Hole](/wiki/The_Black_Hole_(1979_film) "The Black Hole (1979 film)")* * [Hugo Award](/wiki/Hugo_Award "Hugo Award")   1980 *[Best Dramatic Presentation](/wiki/Hugo_Award_for_Best_Dramatic_Presentation#Best_Dramatic_Presentation "Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation")* – *[The Black Hole](/wiki/The_Black_Hole_(1979_film) "The Black Hole (1979 film)")* |

**Gerry Day** (January 27, 1922 – February 13, 2013) was an American screenwriter. She was also a newspaper reporter for the *Hollywood Citizen News* in the mid-1940s.[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gerry_Day&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Gerald Lallande Day was born in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), [California](/wiki/California "California"), the daughter of Ruthy and Lenox Day.[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Two-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Three-3) She was given her name not because her parents had wanted a boy but due to their Southern family name traditions.

Her father was the [organist](/wiki/Organist "Organist") for the [Grauman's Egyptian Theatre](/wiki/Grauman%27s_Egyptian_Theatre "Grauman's Egyptian Theatre") on [Hollywood Boulevard](/wiki/Hollywood_Boulevard "Hollywood Boulevard"). She watched [Howard Hughes](/wiki/Howard_Hughes "Howard Hughes") film the [miniature](/wiki/Miniature_effect "Miniature effect") [dogfights](/wiki/Dogfight "Dogfight") for the 1930 film *[Hell's Angels](/wiki/Hell%27s_Angels_(film) "Hell's Angels (film)")* in a lot behind her childhood home. [Lana Turner](/wiki/Lana_Turner "Lana Turner") was her escort and gave her a campus tour when Day first enrolled at [Hollywood High School](/wiki/Hollywood_High_School "Hollywood High School"). [Orson Welles](/wiki/Orson_Welles "Orson Welles") once hypnotized her in his magic act at the [Hollywood Canteen](/wiki/Hollywood_Canteen "Hollywood Canteen").[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gerry_Day&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Day later attended and graduated from [UCLA](/wiki/University_of_California,_Los_Angeles "University of California, Los Angeles") in 1944. She became a newspaper reporter for the *Hollywood Citizen News*, filing [obituaries](/wiki/Obituary "Obituary") and writing reviews of plays. She took a [radio drama](/wiki/Radio_drama "Radio drama")-writing class, which led to her writing [spec scripts](/wiki/Spec_script "Spec script") for some local television programs at the time. Producer [Frank Wisbar](/wiki/Frank_Wisbar "Frank Wisbar") would later teach her how to write [teleplays](/wiki/Teleplay "Teleplay") for his *[Fireside Theater](/wiki/Fireside_Theater "Fireside Theater")*, and she would later work for [Screen Gems](/wiki/Screen_Gems "Screen Gems") producer [Irving Starr](/wiki/Irving_Starr "Irving Starr") and *[Ford Theatre](/wiki/Ford_Theatre "Ford Theatre")*.[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)

In the 1950s, Day would take a break and tour around Europe, while her mother at home would write her saying that she would love watching the new television shows featuring horses — *[Rawhide](/wiki/Rawhide_(TV_series) "Rawhide (TV series)")*, *[Have Gun Will Travel](/wiki/Have_Gun_Will_Travel "Have Gun Will Travel")* and *[Wagon Train](/wiki/Wagon_Train "Wagon Train")*. In 1959, Day, who loved horses, met with *Wagon Train* producer Howard Christie, who let her write her own scripts, as well as [doctor](/wiki/Script_doctor "Script doctor") others, for the series. Day would also be an unofficial [bookie](/wiki/Bookmaker "Bookmaker") for the series' crew, betting on horse races for them, and eventually becoming part owner of a racehorse.[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)

She would become well-versed in the [Western](/wiki/Western_(genre) "Western (genre)") genre, writing for such series as *[Here Come the Brides](/wiki/Here_Come_the_Brides "Here Come the Brides")*, *[The High Chaparral](/wiki/The_High_Chaparral "The High Chaparral")*, *[Tate](/wiki/Tate_(TV_series) "Tate (TV series)")*, *[Temple Houston](/wiki/Temple_Houston_(TV_series) "Temple Houston (TV series)")*, *[The Virginian](/wiki/The_Virginian_(TV_series) "The Virginian (TV series)")*, *[The Big Valley](/wiki/The_Big_Valley "The Big Valley")*, *[The Outcasts](/wiki/The_Outcasts_(U.S._TV_series) "The Outcasts (U.S. TV series)")*, *[The New Land](/wiki/The_New_Land_(TV_series) "The New Land (TV series)")*, and *[Little House on the Prairie](/wiki/Little_House_on_the_Prairie_(TV_series) "Little House on the Prairie (TV series)")*. She also wrote for such other series as *[Medical Center](/wiki/Medical_Center_(TV_series) "Medical Center (TV series)")*; *[My Friend Tony](/wiki/My_Friend_Tony "My Friend Tony")*; *[Judd, for the Defense](/wiki/Judd,_for_the_Defense "Judd, for the Defense")*; *[Peyton Place](/wiki/Peyton_Place_(TV_series) "Peyton Place (TV series)")*; *[Marcus Welby, M.D.](/wiki/Marcus_Welby,_M.D. "Marcus Welby, M.D.")*; *[Dr. Kildare](/wiki/Dr._Kildare_(TV_series) "Dr. Kildare (TV series)")*; *[Court Martial](/wiki/Court_Martial_(TV_series) "Court Martial (TV series)")*; *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* and *[Dennis the Menace](/wiki/Dennis_the_Menace_(1986_TV_series) "Dennis the Menace (1986 TV series)")*. She sometimes used the pseudonym Jon Gerald.[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)[[3]](#cite_note-Three-3)

Day co-wrote scripts with [Bethel Leslie](/wiki/Bethel_Leslie "Bethel Leslie"), an actress and head writer for the long-running [soap opera](/wiki/Soap_opera "Soap opera") *[The Secret Storm](/wiki/The_Secret_Storm "The Secret Storm")*. They would write scripts for series such as *[Bracken's World](/wiki/Bracken%27s_World "Bracken's World")*, *[Matt Helm](/wiki/Matt_Helm_(TV_series) "Matt Helm (TV series)")*, *[The New Adventures of Perry Mason](/wiki/The_New_Adventures_of_Perry_Mason "The New Adventures of Perry Mason")*, *[Electra Woman and Dyna Girl](/wiki/Electra_Woman_and_Dyna_Girl "Electra Woman and Dyna Girl")* and *[Barnaby Jones](/wiki/Barnaby_Jones "Barnaby Jones")*.[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)

## Awards and honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gerry_Day&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards and honors")]

In 1967, Day was nominated for a [Writers Guild of America Award](/wiki/Writers_Guild_of_America_Award_for_Television:_Episodic_Drama "Writers Guild of America Award for Television: Episodic Drama") for the *[Court Martial](/wiki/Court_Martial_(TV_series) "Court Martial (TV series)")* episode "Judge Them Gently". In 1980, Day was co-nominated for both a [Saturn Award](/wiki/7th_Saturn_Awards#Best_Writing "7th Saturn Awards") and a [Hugo Award](/wiki/Hugo_Award_for_Best_Dramatic_Presentation#Best_Dramatic_Presentation "Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation") for the screenplay of the film *[The Black Hole](/wiki/The_Black_Hole_(1979_film) "The Black Hole (1979 film)")*.

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gerry_Day&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

A devout [Catholic](/wiki/Catholicism "Catholicism"), Day became a [Eucharist](/wiki/Eucharist_in_the_Catholic_Church "Eucharist in the Catholic Church") minister in her church. She also raised [foster children](/wiki/Foster_care "Foster care") and supported equestrian causes. She died on February 13, 2013, after a long battle with cancer.[[1]](#cite_note-One-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Two-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Three-3)

She was interred on February 19 at the [San Fernando Mission Cemetery](/wiki/San_Fernando_Mission_Cemetery "San Fernando Mission Cemetery") in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles").[[2]](#cite_note-Two-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Three-3)
